description: A mortise cut to its mark and a sentence with one exact scope can both be 'kiro', since each keeps unintended variation narrow. 'telua' (accurate) agrees with a reference or fact; something can be precise and still miss its mark.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then draw the tongue back and round the lips for true-mid back 'o'. Stress falls on 'ki'.
sound_symbolism: The complete closure of 'k' and the brief contact of 'r' give the two syllables crisp edges. The compact word recalls a line cut close to its mark.
usage_notes: 'kiro' applies to dimensions, wording, boundaries, or repeated results with a narrow spread. Exact values and tolerances remain in their source record when the original form matters.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A flowing iron curve still depends on an exact joint and a measured fit. 'kiro' names the precision that lets the line survive making.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: cleanly controlled detail or execution in a made form
semantic_domains.cognition: narrow scope, exact expression, or little unintended variation
